contract_meta.enhancement: Added CDI discovery phase, evidence requirements, and anti-drift guardrails while preserving all implementation detail
contract_meta.rationale: Research findings (04_ai_integration_pattern.md) applied: selective discovery before changes, evidence-backed validation, stack compliance enforcement

project.name: executor-mvp
project.goal: Fix critical UI perception issues with discovery-first approach and evidence-backed validation
project.scope: Discovery task + Wins A1-A3 + Evidence collection
project.work_profile: Discovery phase + Frontend JavaScript, HTML, CSS - no backend changes

execution_model.description: Agent performs discovery to map integration points, then implements with detailed guidance, then provides evidence

observability.description: CDI-enhanced observability: discovery decisions + implementation trace + evidence collection

tasks.title: Verify Phase 4 completion + stack compliance
tasks.description: Confirm Phase 4 complete and verify ai-stack.json compliance before discovery

actions.on_missing: HALT - ai-stack.json must exist before Phase A execution

evaluation.intent: Verify Phase 4 complete and stack lock exists
evaluation.success_criteria: All Phase 4 artifacts exist, ai-stack.json present, tests pass

tasks.title: Discovery Phase - Map UI Integration Points
tasks.description: Discover and document integration points for all 3 wins before making any changes

actions.purpose: Identify execution flow and where to hook success/loading/error
Find where results are currently rendered (to replace with card)

actions.requirement: For each win, identify exact integration point (file + line + function/selector)

win_a1_integration.function: executeRequest (specific line where resultEl.textContent is set)

win_a2_integration.proposed_hook: Replace static text with updateLoadingPhase() call

win_a3_integration.function: catch blocks in executeRequest and startClarificationFlow
win_a3_integration.proposed_hook: Replace error textContent with formatError() HTML

actions.requirement: Create discovery note with code snippets (±10 lines each)
content: - Justification: This is where successful execution results are rendered

validation.reason: Discovery note must be substantial (30+ lines with snippets)

success_criteria: All 3 win integration points identified with exact file/line/function
Code snippets captured for each integration point
Stack compliance verified (TypeScript, no Python, /public only)
Zero breaking changes identified
Discovery JSON and markdown note generated

evaluation.intent: Map integration landscape before making changes
evaluation.success_criteria: Specific integration points documented with evidence
evaluation.on_fail: HALT - Cannot proceed without clear integration plan

tasks.title: Win #A1: Success Card (Replace JSON Dump)
tasks.description: Replace raw JSON output with formatted success card - using integration point from discovery
tasks.work_profile: Frontend JavaScript function, HTML structure, CSS styling

implementation: Function signature: function renderSuccessCard(data)
Detect success: if (!data.ok || !data.files_written) return false
Clear resultEl first
Create div.success-card with:
- div.success-header: icon (✅) + h2 'Project Generated Successfully!'
- div.success-metrics: 3 metric cards (files, tests, time)
- div.file-list: h3 + ul of generated files with icons
- div.action-buttons: primary 'Open Project' link + secondary 'Run Tests' button
- details.raw-json: summary 'View Raw Response' + pre with JSON
Append card to resultEl
Return true to indicate success card rendered

content_spec.location: At integration point identified in PA-DISC

css: .success-card { background: rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(34, 197, 94, 0.3); border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; margin-top: 16px; }
.success-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; }
.success-metrics { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr)); gap: 16px; margin-bottom: 24px; }
.metric { text-align: center; padding: 12px; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); border-radius: 6px; }
.metric-value { display: block; font-size: 28px; font-weight: bold; color: #a7f3d0; }
.file-list li { padding: 6px 0; color: #cbd5e1; font-family: 'Monaco', 'Courier New', monospace; font-size: 14px; }
.action-buttons .btn { padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 6px; text-decoration: none; font-weight: 500; transition: all 0.2s ease; }

success_criteria: renderSuccessCard function exists in script.js
Function called at discovered integration point
Success card shows: header, metrics, file list, action buttons, collapsed JSON
CSS styles make card visually distinct (green theme)
Lint passes, no console errors
JSON still available in collapsed details element

evidence_requirements.integration_snippet: Code showing renderSuccessCard() call at exact location
evidence_requirements.visual_proof: Manual test confirming success card renders correctly

tasks.title: Win #A2: Loading States (Phase-Aware Messages)
tasks.description: Add spinner and phase-aware loading messages - using integration point from discovery
tasks.work_profile: Frontend JavaScript timing, HTML structure, CSS animations
tasks.prerequisite: PA-DISC complete - loading message location identified

implementation: Add module-level variable: let loadingPhaseTimer = null;
Add module-level variable: let loadingPhase = 0;
Function signature: function updateLoadingPhase()
Phases:
Phase 0 (0-10s): 'Analyzing your request...' + 'Reading requirements and preparing plan. (typically 10-20 seconds)'
Phase 1 (10-30s): 'Creating execution plan...' + 'Breaking down into manageable steps. (typically 10-30 seconds)'
Phase 2 (30s+): 'Building your project...' + 'Generating files and running tests. This may take several minutes.'
Update resultEl with spinner HTML + phase message
Return timer reference

content_spec.location: At loading state integration point from PA-DISC

css: .spinner { width: 40px; height: 40px; margin: 0 auto 20px; border: 4px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3); border-top-color: #3b82f6; border-radius: 50%; animation: spin 0.8s linear infinite; }
.loading-hint { color: #94a3b8; font-size: 14px; max-width: 400px; margin: 0 auto; line-height: 1.5; }

success_criteria: updateLoadingPhase function exists and works
Loading message updates every 10 seconds
Spinner animation visible and smooth
Three distinct phases with different messages
Timer cleared when generation completes
No visual jump or flicker between phases

evidence_requirements.integration_snippet: Code showing updateLoadingPhase() implementation and timer setup

tasks.title: Win #A3: Error Message Formatting
tasks.description: Convert technical errors to user-friendly messages - using error handling points from discovery
tasks.work_profile: Frontend JavaScript error handling, HTML structure, CSS styling
tasks.prerequisite: PA-DISC complete - error handling locations identified

implementation: Function signature: function formatError(error)
Define errorMap object with common errors:
'Failed to fetch': {title: 'Connection Error', message: 'Unable to connect to server', action: 'Check server is running: npm run dev'}
'ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED': {title: 'Server Not Running', message: 'Backend service not responding', action: 'Start server: npm run dev'}
'timeout': {title: 'Request Timeout', message: 'Operation took too long', action: 'Try simpler request or check logs'}
'NetworkError': {title: 'Network Error', message: 'Network connection lost', action: 'Check internet connection'}
Match error string against errorMap keys
If match found, return formatted error card HTML
If no match, return generic friendly error with technical details collapsed
HTML structure: div.error-card with header, message, action, details

content_spec.location: At error handling integration point from PA-DISC
At error handling integration point from PA-DISC

css: .error-card { background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.3); border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; margin-top: 16px; }
.error-card .error-header { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 16px; }
.error-card .error-action { color: #cbd5e1; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); padding: 12px; border-radius: 6px; margin-bottom: 16px; font-family: 'Monaco', monospace; font-size: 14px; }
.error-card pre { margin-top: 12px; color: #94a3b8; font-size: 12px; max-height: 200px; overflow-y: auto; }

success_criteria: formatError function exists and handles common errors
Error card shows: title, message, actionable steps
Technical details collapsed by default
All error paths use formatError (executeRequest, startClarificationFlow)
CSS makes errors distinct but not alarming (red theme, not aggressive)
Unknown errors still get friendly wrapping

evidence_requirements.integration_snippet: Code showing formatError() calls at discovered error handling points
evidence_requirements.error_scenarios: Manual test of connection error, timeout, unknown error

tasks.title: Collect and validate all evidence artifacts
tasks.description: Generate SBOM, validate contract schema, compile evidence for PA-GATE
success_criteria: Evidence JSON exists with all required fields
SBOM artifact generated
Contract validates against schema
All test runs documented with exit codes

tasks.title: Phase A validation gate (CDI-enhanced)
tasks.description: Final validation with evidence requirements and stack compliance

actions.cmd: grep -q 'renderSuccessCard' public/script.js && grep -q 'updateLoadingPhase' public/script.js && grep -q 'formatError' public/script.js
grep -q 'success-card' public/styles.css && grep -q 'spinner' public/styles.css && grep -q 'error-card' public/styles.css

tests: Generate successful project - verify success card appears with metrics, files, buttons
Watch loading states - verify spinner animates, messages update every 10s
Stop server and try execute - verify friendly error message with connection guidance
All UI changes look professional, no raw JSON visible on success
No console errors during any flow

evaluation.intent: Confirm Phase A complete with CDI evidence and stack compliance
evaluation.success_criteria: All wins work, all evidence artifacts present, gates pass, stack compliant
evaluation.on_pass: Phase A COMPLETE - CDI pattern established. Ready for Phase B (Trust Engine)
evaluation.on_fail: HALT - Fix issues before declaring phase complete

discovery_first.description: PA-DISC must complete successfully before any win implementation
discovery_first.enforcement: Wins reference integration points from discovery
discovery_first.rationale: Selective discovery prevents full-repo analysis and ensures precision

win_isolation.description: Each win must be complete, tested, and verified before next
win_isolation.implementation: Visual verification + evidence collection after each win

evidence_backed.description: All claims must be backed by artifacts

PA-GATE.on_fail: HALT execution. Do not proceed to Phase B.
PA-GATE.evidence_check: All evidence artifacts must exist and validate

fallback_policies.discovery_fails: HALT - Cannot implement without integration plan
fallback_policies.evidence_missing: HALT - Cannot pass gate without evidence

release_actions.cmd: git commit -m "feat(phaseA): UI baseline with CDI - discovery, wins, evidence"

ai_executor_guidelines.discovery_mandatory: PA-DISC must complete before any win. Document exact integration points with code snippets.
ai_executor_guidelines.no_assumptions: Never assume file structure. Always discover, then implement.
ai_executor_guidelines.evidence_first: Every claim must be backed by an artifact. No 'trust me' statements.
ai_executor_guidelines.win_isolation: Complete and verify each win fully before proceeding.
ai_executor_guidelines.stack_compliance: Verify against ai-stack.json at every step. Halt if violated.
ai_executor_guidelines.gate_enforcement: PA-GATE must pass before declaring phase complete.
ai_executor_guidelines.quality_over_speed: Ship perfect or never. No shortcuts.

governance_note.for_human: Phase A enhanced with CDI pattern from research findings. Discovery phase ensures no assumptions, evidence requirements ensure proof, stack compliance prevents drift. All implementation detail preserved. This is the 'ship perfect' version.
